generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T POLYPORUS OFFICINALIS. [This includes the first proving by Dr. Burt, published in this work under Boletus .] Polyporus officinalis, Fries. Natural order , Fungi. Common names , Larch agaric. Preparation , Tincture of the fresh plant. Authorities. 1 , Dr. Burt, Am. Hom. Obs., 1868, p. 116, took 25 grains of 3d dec. trit., at 10 A.M. and 9 P.M. (first day); 20 grains, crude, 10 A.M. (second day); 30 grains (third day); 40 grains (fourth day); 32 grains (fifth day); 50 grains (sixth day); 1 a , same, second proving, continued four days, the first day took 115 grains; 2 , F. A. Lord, Am. Hom. Obs., 1868, p. 60, repeated dose of 1st dec. trit., for five days; doses, 1 to 20 grains; 2 a , same, one dose of 40 grains, 1st dec. trit.; 2 b , same, took crude, 10 grains, at 6 and 9 P.M., first day, at noon second day; morning, 20 grains, at noon fifth day; 40 grains, sixth day; 2 c , same, one dose of 20 grains, crude; 2 d , same, 40 grains, crude; 3 , R. C. Smedley, took 3d dec. trit. for three days, 5 to 100-grain doses, then 1st dec. trit. for two days, 80 and 100-grain doses, then crude, 20 to 50-grain doses for two days; 4 , J. B. Wood, took 1st trit. for six days, 1 to 100-grain doses; 4 a , same, subsequently took crude, 5 to 20-grain doses; 5 , E. Cooley, took crude, 10 to 25-grain doses; 6 , E. Miller, took 1st trit. 50 grains, first and second days; 25 grains, third day; 60 drops, 1st dil., fourth day; 100 drops of tincture, sixth day. MIND. ► *Mind gloomy and desponding (second day), [5] , [6] . ► *Gloomy and desponding, and even morose (ninth day), [6] . ► *Very sad and gloomy , [1a] . ► Low-spirited and irritable (sixth day), [2b] . ► Very dull and depressed all day, [2d] . ► Feel dull and indisposed to exertion (seventh day); for five days succeeding this I had not my usual appetite or spirits; suffered from a general feeling of not being quite well, but without any special describable pains or symptoms; it was precisely the feeling of one who is said to be bilious, [2b] . HEAD. ► My head is feeling very light and hollow, with deep frontal headache, in the morning (sixth day), [1] . ► Head feeling light (seventh day), [1] . ► *Light dizzy feeling in the head (third day), [6] . [10.] ► Slight feeling of fulness in the head (sixth day), [2b] . ► Awoke with a dull pain in the head, which continued all day; thought it might be due to a glass of ale drank the evening before (third day), awoke with a dull pain in the head, with sensation of soreness in the brain, increased by stooping or shaking the head; this headache is precisely similar to that experienced on the third, which I attributed to the glass of ale; I now believe that the pain at that time was caused by the Polyporus (seventh day), [2b] . ► *Dull headache (third day), [4a] . ► Dull feeling in head (tenth day), [3] . ► *Dull aching pain in the head (after ten hours, sixth day), [6] . ► Severe headache, aggravated by motion (after seven hours, third day), [6] . ► Pain in the head, jaws, and teeth (fifth day), [6] . ► Head ached severely all night (third night), [1] . ► *Headache, qualmishness , relieved by eructations (third day), [4a] . ► Most of the head symptoms greatly aggravated (after four hours, fourth day), [5] . [20.] ► Severe semilateral headache (ninth day), [5] . ► Forehead. ► *Dull frontal headache , lasting all day (after half an hour), [1] . ► Continued dulness of the forehead, with slight aching (first day), [4a] . ► Dull frontal headache (third day), [1] ; (tenth day), [3] . ► Dull frontal headache (fourth day); in the evening (fifth day), [3] . ► Dulness of the forehead (second day), [1] . ► Dull, heavy pain in the forehead, worse on motion (second night), [6] . ► Heaviness of the forehead (fourth day), [4a] . ► At 8 A.M., violent frontal headache; very much aggravated by walking; head feels light and hollow (fourth day), [1] . [30.] ► Hard frontal headache (fifth day), [1] . ► Violent frontal headache, with an extremely light, hollow feeling of the head; the headache is greatly aggravated by walking (sixth day), [1] . ► Deep frontal headache for the last forty-eight hours (seventh day), [6] . ► Frontal headache, aggravated by motion (second day), [3] . ► Continuous oppressive headache in forehead and temples, all day (sixth day), [3] . ► In the evening tight feeling in the forehead, with slight headache (second day); tightness of the forehead as yesterday (third day), [4] . ► Tightness across the forehead, with dull headache (fifth day), [4] . ► Momentary twinge over the left eyebrow (sixth day), [2b] . ► Temples. ► Dull pains through temples (second day), [3] . ► Dull heavy pains through temples and in forehead (ninth day), [3] . ► Aching soreness through temples (seventh day), [3] . [40.] ► Lacerating pain in the right temple, at 9.40 P.M., [2c] . ► Neuralgic pain in the temples (second day), [5] . ► Neuralgic pains in the temples, face, and teeth, lasting four hours (fourth day), [5] . ► Some pain in the temples (ninth day), [5] . ► Frequent neuralgic pains in the temples (second day), [6] . ► Frequent cutting pain in the temples (after ten hours), [6] . ► Severe pain in the left temple, of a neuralgic character (third day), [6] . ► A few sharp pains through temples (first day), [3] . ► Sharp pains in the temples (after four hours, sixth day), [6] . ► Occiput. ► A little soreness in the occipital region followed the shooting pain up the back (third day), [2] . EYE. [50.] ► Eyeballs ache severely (sixth day), [1] . ► Deep drawing pain in the eyeballs (second day), [5] . FACE. ► Face of a bluish color (fourth day), [5] . ► Pain in the jaws, teeth, and head (fifth day), [6] . MOUTH. ► Teeth and Gums. ► Teeth feel as if they were being forced out of their sockets (fourth day), [5] . ► Teeth and gums became very sore, [1a] . ► Gums and teeth are all sore (seventh day), [1] . ► Gums sore (fourth day), [3] . ► Tongue. ► *Tongue coated white (third day), [6] . ► *Yellow tinge of tongue (ninth day), [3] . [60.] ► *Yellow coating of tongue (tenth day), [3] ; (eighth day), [6] . ► *Tongue is coated thickly, yellow (sixth and seventh days), [1] . ► Tongue coated light brown (fourth day), [6] . ► Tongue red at the tip, and coated yellow at the base (fifth day), [1] . ► Prickling in the end of the tongue, which soon passed away (after one hour), [2a] . ► Taste. ► *Bitter taste in the mouth (sixth day), [1] ; (eighth day), [6] . ► Bitter, coppery taste in the mouth (seventh day), [1] . ► Flat, metallic, or coppery taste (ninth day), [3] . ► Constant coppery taste in the mouth, [1a] . ► Slight coppery taste in the mouth and throat (after four hours, fourth day), [5] . [70.] ► Flat, bitter taste in the mouth (fifth day), [1] ; (third day), [5] . ► Sweet and bitter taste in the mouth (fifth day), [6] . ► Sweetish, nauseous taste (tenth day), [3] . THROAT. ► Rawness and scraping in the throat, all the afternoon and evening (sixth day), [2b] . ► Feeling in the fauces as if I would vomit, but no nausea (third day), [1] . STOMACH. ► Appetite and Thirst. ► Appetite invigorated (fourth day), [3] . ► Very little appetite (ninth day); little appetite (tenth day), [3] . ► Not so much appetite for breakfast as common (fourth day), [2] . ► No appetite for supper, which is contrary to my usual habit (sixth day), [2b] . ► At 1 P.M., poor appetite for dinner (eleventh day); slight diminution of appetite for forty-eight hours, [2c] . [80.] ► *Loss of appetite , [1a] . ► Desire for sour things, which have always relieved the symptoms (tenth day), [3] . ► *Much thirst (third day), [4a] . ► Eructations and Heartburn. ► Eructations (third day), [4a] . ► Heartburn, with acid risings (sixth day), [2b] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea (after twenty minutes, sixth day), [2b] . ► Frequent spells of nausea, lasting but a moment (third day), [1] . ► Nausea at the stomach (after four hours), [5] . ► Nausea and slight vomiting of a sour watery fluid, intensely bitter (after four hours, fourth day), [5] . ► Some nausea, particularly in throat (second day); nausea at times (third day); particularly in throat and fauces (sixth day), [3] . [90.] ► Felt sick at stomach (ninth day), [3] . ► Qualmishness, with the loose stool (second day); qualmishness, headache, relieved by eructations (third day), [4a] . ► Stomach. ► Tonic effects, invigorated digestion (third, fourth, and fifth days), [3] . ► Effects decidedly tonic (fifth day); instead of being tonic as before, its effects were now enervating (ninth day), [3] . ► Deep distress in the epigastrium, lasting all day, with dull pains in the right hypochondrium, and sharp pains in the epigastrium; aggravated by walking (after half an hour), [1] . ► Dull, aching, burning distress in the epigastrium and whole right hypochondrium (third day), [1] . ► Dull, aching distress in the epigastric and umbilical regions (third night), [1] . ► Great burning distress in the region of the stomach and liver, extending down to the umbilicus, with urgent desire for stool; the same distress after a stool; at 10 A.M. it is very severe , with urgent desire for stool; continues after a stool (fourth day), [1] . ► The most terrible distress between the stomach and navel, lasted all the afternoon; could not get relief in any position; accompanied by great faintness and high fever (fifth day), [1] . [100.] ► Burning pain in the stomach, with dragging pain in the hypochondrium (first and second days), [6] . ► Burning in the epigastrium (after four hours, sixth day), [6] . ► In evening, feeling as if lump in stomach (sixth day), [3] . ► Coldness of stomach, followed by burning (after several hours, seventh day), [3] . ► Dull aching pain in the stomach and bowels (ninth day), [5] . ► Pain in the epigastrium (after four hours), [5] . ► Pain in stomach (after four hours, and eighth day), [5] ; (fourth day), [6] . ► Pain in stomach and liver (third day), [5] . ► Pain in the stomach and bowels, almost unbearable (after four hours, fourth day), [5] . ► Woke at midnight with hard, cutting pains in the epigastrium (second night); at 5 A.M., awoke with the same cutting pains in the epigastric region, and distress in the umbilicus, with great desire for stool (third morning), [1] . ► Severe sharp cutting pains in stomach and bowels (sixth day), [3] . [110.] ► During the day very sharp pains in the stomach, almost constant borborygmus (seventh day), [3] . ► Sharp, cutting, lancinating pains in the stomach (ninth day), [3] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. ► Dull, heavy, aching pains in the region of the right lobe of the liver, after the morning stool (third day), [1] . ► Dull, heavy, drawing pains in the region of the right lobe of the liver, extending through to the dorsal region (third day), [1] . ► Heavy dragging pains in the right lobe of the liver (third night), [1] . ► 1 P.M., have had constant and severe aching distress in the right lobe of the liver, lower part of the epigastrium and upper part of the umbilicus (fifth day), [1] . ► Distress in the right hypochondrium and umbilicus (fifth day), [1] . ► A full inspiration produces sharp, stabbing pains in the right lobe of the liver and lower dorsal region (fifth day), [1] . ► Great distress in the region of the liver and small intestines, with the stool, at 9 A.M. (sixth day), [1] . ► Dull aching distress in the right lobe of liver and umbilicus, with great desire for stool (sixth day), [1] . [120.] ► Pain in the liver and stomach (third day), [5] . ► Dragging pain in the hypochondrium, with burning pain in the stomach (first and second days), [6] . ► Dragging in the right hypochondrium (after four hours, sixth day), [6] . ► Dull aching pain in the liver (after ten hours, sixth day), [6] . ► Dragging pain in the liver (sixth day), [6] . ► Severe pain in the liver (sixth day), [5] . ► Pain in the liver (eighth day), [5] . ► Hard pain in the region of the spleen and left epigastric region (after three hours, fourth day), [6] . ► Sharp pain in the spleen, liver, and bowels (third and fifth days), [6] . [130.] ► Sharp pain in the region of the spleen (after four hours, sixth day), [6] . ► Frequent sharp pains in the spleen (after ten hours, sixth day), [6] . ► Umbilicus. ► Severe distress in the umbilical and hypogastric region, with great desire for stool (third day), [1] . ► Slight uneasiness in subumbilical region, which gradually increased, with inclination to go to stool, having about an hour previously had my usual morning stool; during the stool which followed, a shooting pain extended upon each side of the umbilicus, towards the iliac, and then seemed to extend to the floor of the pelvis, in a ring around the rectum; these pains were not very severe, abated somewhat, but did not entirely disappear after stool (third day), [2] . ► Distress in the region of the umbilicus (after six hours, second day), [6] . ► Dull pain in the region of the umbilicus for the last forty-eight hours (seventh day), [6] . ► Severe pain in the regions of the umbilicus, transverse and descending colon, all day long, almost unbearable (fifth day), [5] . ► Pain in the umbilical region (after four hours, and third day), [3] . ► Sharp cutting pain in the umbilicus (first day), [6] . ► Cutting pain in the umbilicus (third day), [5] . [140.] ► Stools preceded by sharp pain in the region of the umbilicus (fifth day), [5] . ► Sharp pain in the umbilical and hypogastric regions (after ten hours, sixth day), [6] . ► General Abdomen. ► Rumbling in the bowels (third day), [6] . ► Loud borborygmus (first day), [1] . ► Dull aching distress in the whole abdomen, with loud borborygmus (third day), [1] . ► Great rumbling in the bowels (seventh day), [1] . ► Rumbling in bowels, as from fermentation (ninth day), [3] . ► Emission of fetid flatulence, in the evening (fifth day), [3] . ► Much flatus in bowels, and feeling as if diarrha would set in (sixth day), [3] . ► Emission of flatus (ninth day), [3] . [150.] ► Burning pain in the region of the transverse colon (after six hours, and second day), [6] . ► Pain in the bowels (fourth day), [6] . ► Pain in the region of the transverse colon, and in the descending colon (third day), [5] . ► Bowels very painful to touch (fourth day), [5] . ► Pain in the bowels and stomach, almost unbearable (after four hours, fourth day), [5] . ► Sharp cutting pain in the bowels before stools (fifth day), [5] . ► Pain in the bowels (eighth day), [5] . ► Dull aching pain in the bowels and stomach (ninth day), [5] . ► At 3 P.M., lacerating pains, here and there, throughout the abdomen, for about five minutes; the pains then became located in the hypogastric region, and continued one hour, with slight inclination to go to stool; the pains then became easier, though they had not entirely passed away at tea-time; felt again ten minutes after a stool, in the lower portion of the abdomen, extending to the right hypochondrium, at 9.40 P.M., [2c] . ► Uneasiness of the bowels (first day), [4a] . [160.] ► Slight aching uneasiness in the whole abdomen, with slight pain all night (second day), [4a] . ► Continued pain over the whole abdomen (third day), [4a] . ► Uneasiness in the abdomen, during the night (fourth day), [4a] . ► Some uneasiness in bowels, from increased vermicular action (fourth and fifth days), [3] . ► Slight uneasiness, as if I would have a stool; this soon passed away (after one hour), [2a] . ► Slight uneasiness in the bowels after retiring (fourth night), [2] . ► Twice during the day had slight pain in the bowels (general), (third day), [4] . ► Sensation as if diarrha would come on (fifth day), [4] . ► Uneasiness of the abdomen, with looseness of the bowels (sixth day), [4] . ► Very sudden and severe distress in the hypogastric region, followed by papescent stools, stools very light-colored (after four hours, sixth day), [6] . [170.] ► Soon after the chilliness felt a slight momentary pain in hypogastric region; in a few minutes experienced similar pains in both hypochondria (sixth day), [2b] . ► This morning have a dull aching pain over the whole lower abdomen (fourth day), [4] . ► Uneasiness in the lower abdomen all day, as if diarrha would come on (fourth day), [4] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Pain in the anus (third day), [5] . ► Severe pain in the anus (third day), [6] . ► Unpleasant sensation, not amounting to pain, about the anus; not felt after 1 P.M. (third day), [2] . ► Desire for stool, with much flatulence (third day), [4a] . ► Slight disposition to go to stool, at 10.30 A.M., soon passed off (fifth day), [2] . ► A disposition to go to school, with some pain in the anus (after four hours), [5] . ► Disposition to go to stool (third day), [5] . [180.] ► Disposition to strain when at stool (fifth day), [5] . STOOL. ► Loose stool , at 6 A.M.; soft mushy stool, mixed with mucus, at 6 P.M., the medicine having acted as a mild cathartic in eight hours (second day); soft, mushy stool, at 5 A.M., followed by dull, heavy aching pains in the region of the right lobe of the liver; after 8 P.M., stool of a dark color, mixed with bile and mucus, followed by a dull aching distress in the dorsal region, extending up to the shoulders, on both sides (third day); at 6 A.M., very black-colored stool, mixed with bile and mucus; before the stool I voided about 1 drachm of white mucus; after 8 A.M., stool that ran a stream from the bowels, of bile, mucus, and black fecal matter, followed by burning distress in region of stomach and liver, extending down to the umbilicus; at 10 A.M., copious stool of water and black fecal matter, with great desire to strain after stool, followed by same symptoms as before; it will be seen by this experiment that 40 grains of the Polyporus, well triturated, will act as a violent hydragogue cathartic in two hours, if it is taken on an empty stomach (fourth day); in the morning, hard, black, lumpy stool, mixed with bile and mucus; at 1 P.M., soft, black, fetid, papescent stool, followed by the most terrible distress between the stomach and navel (fifth day); in the morning, dark papescent stool; mushy stool at 9 A.M., with great distress in the region of the liver and small intestines; at 6 P.M., papescent stool, followed by about one teaspoonful of blood, with severe tenesmus (sixth day); soft, mushy stool (seventh day); papescent, dark-colored stool, without pain (eighth day); my bowels have been loose every day, with from one to three stools a day, without pain; all have been of a dark color; to-day my stool in natural, and I am feeling well (thirteenth day), [1] . ► Loose stool, with qualmishness (second day); at 9 A.M., at 3.30 P.M., copious fluid stool, expelled with great force, and another of the same character at 4 (third day); at loose, yellow stool, with pain afterwards, at 9 A.M.; loose stool, with pain, at 6 P.M. (fourth day); bowels loose (fifth day); loose stool, with tenesmus, at 2 and 3 P.M. (sixth day); six days have elapsed since the last dose, and my bowels have not become regular yet (ninth day), [4a] . ► Copious, soft, mushy stool, followed by slight uneasiness in the umbilical region, at 3.30 P.M. (sixth day); scanty stool, at 9 A.M. (seventh day), [2b] . ► Soft, mushy stool, unattended with pain, at 9.30 P.M., [2c] . ► Soft mushy stool, at 9 P.M., similar to the one in the morning, but without the accompanying symptoms (third day); no stool at the usual time, but a mushy one at 9.30 A.M., preceded by the same pains in the lower portion of the abdomen, which immediately passed off after stool (fourth day); usual stool after breakfast (fifth day), [2] . ► Slight motion of the bowels (fifth day), [4] . ► Two stools, in the morning (fourth day), [6] . ► Stools the first part of the day dry and lumpy, but in the evening were quite frequent, thin, watery, and very yellow (fifth day), [6] . ► Very light-colored, papescent stools, followed distress in hypogastric region (after four hours, sixth day), [6] . [190.] ► Soft papescent stools during the last forty-eight hours (seventh day), [6] . ► Copious and watery stools, accompanied with severe pains (fourth day), [5] . ► Stools dark, dry, and lumpy, followed with stools mixed with bile and mucus; stools mixed with blood; stools thin and yellow (fifth day), [5] . ► One copious stool, at 3 P.M. (ninth day), [5] . ► The stools were not so black, and all contained more or less undigested food, and were mixed with water; also a number contained something that had the appearance of oil; it was in drops, from the size of a common new cent down to small drops; the color was clear and white; was it oil or not?, [1a] . ► Passage of dry, hard fæces (tenth day), [3] . ► Bowels costive (ninth day), [3] ; (eight day), [6] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Increased urination (ninth day), [3] . ► Frequent passage of high-colored urine, with pain in the region of the kidneys (third day), [5] . ► *Urine diminished and high-colored (sixth day), [5] . [200.] ► *Urine very high-colored and scanty (fifth day), [1] . ► Increased secretion of urine (sixth day), [3] . ► Increased desire to urinate (seventh day), [3] . ► Frequent desire to urinate (tenth day), [3] . ► Frequent urination during the night (first night), [4] . ► Urine acid and profuse, light-colored (fifth day), [4] . ► Passed urine freely at 10 A.M., 12 M., 2, 4, 6, and 9 P.M.; pale almost colorless; desire to pass urine was constant (second day); constant desire to urinate; passed urine profusely, at 10 A.M., 12 M, and 2 P.M. (third day); profuse urination (fourth day), [4a] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Soon after getting into bed, at 10 P.M., a sharp pain in the glans penis, very severe, [2c] . CHEST. ► Pain in the left lung (third day), [6] . ► Sharp darting pains in the left lung, with slight suffocating feeling, causing me to rise up in bed, lasting four hours (fourth day), [5] . [210.] ► Dull aching in the left lung, extending through the left shoulder-blade (ninth day), [5] . ► Pain in the ribs, aggravated by motion (after six hours), [6] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Sharp piercing pain in the region of the heart, aggravated by motion (after six hours), [6] . ► *Pulse 110, full and soft (fifth day), [1] . ► Pulse 100, at 6 P.M.; 96 at 9 P.M. (sixth day), [1] . ► Pulse from 90 to 105 (first night), [5] . ► Pulse 90, full and wiry (ninth day), [3] . ► Pulse 100, full and soft (seventh day), [6] . NECK AND BACK. ► Neck. ► Slight drawing pains in the left side of the neck, after getting into bed, at 10 P.M., [2c] . ► A slight pain shot up the back of the neck, from between the shoulder-blades, followed by a little soreness of the occipital region, with disposition to throw the head back; this soon disappeared (third day), [2] . ► Back. [220.] ► *Severe backache (fourth day), [6] . ► Severe backache; back is very stiff; can hardly rise up after sitting down a few minutes (seventh day), [1] . ► *Dull aching pain in the back and hips (third day), [6] . ► Dull pain in the back (after four hours, sixth day), [6] . ► Dull aching pain in the back (after ten hours, sixth day), [6] . ► 5 P.M., back very painful and stiff (seventh day), [6] . ► Dull and aching pain in the back and limbs (second day), [5] . ► Drawing pains, several times through the day, in the back and legs (first day), [4] . ► Dorsal. ► Rheumatic pains in the spine and limbs, lasting four hours (fourth day), [5] . ► Dull aching distress in the dorsal region, extending up to the shoulders, on both sides, after a stool (third day), [1] . [230.] ► Great aching distress in the dorsal and lumbar region (sixth day), [1] . ► Lumbar. ► Hard aching distress in the lumbar region (third night), [1] . ► Dull aching distress in the lumbar region (sixth day), [1] . ► Dull pains in the lumbar region (third day), [1] . ► Pain and weakness in the small of the back (sixth day), [5] . ► Dull pain in the small of the back (sixth day), [6] . ► Aching in the small of the back all day (fifth day), [4a] . EXTREMITIES. ► Weakness in the arms and legs, can scarcely walk or stand (sixth day), [5] . ► *Severe pain in the arms and legs (sixth day), [5] . ► Rheumatic pains in the limbs and spine, lasting four hours (fourth day), [5] . [240.] ► Great aching distress in all the joints (fifth and sixth days), [1] . ► Severe aching in all the large joints, with great lassitude (sixth day), [1] . ► Rheumatic pains in all the joints (third day), [6] . ► Severe pain in all the joints of the extremities (third day), [6] . ► 5 P.M., have had since noon severe pains in all the joints of the upper and lower extremities (seventh day), [6] . ► All the symptoms began to subside except the rheumatic pains in the limbs and joints, they being almost unbearable (eighth day); rheumatic pains still continue (ninth day); pains still continue, but are light in comparison to what they were on the ninth day (twentieth day), [6] . ► While riding in the country I felt a sharp pain, for about a minute, exactly where the pulse is in the right wrist, and seeming to extend along the course of the artery, for about two inches above the joint; the pain was quite severe and very sudden in coming on; about like a shock from a galvanic battery (after half an hour); soon after, while still riding, I felt a similar pain for about half minute, in the right shoulder, seeming to be in the joint, and thence outward to the deltoid muscle; at 2 P.M., while writing, felt a pain precisely like that experienced in the wrist just above the elbow-joint, and external to the tendon of the biceps muscle; lasted but a moment; in a few seconds felt a similar pain in the left knee, on the internal side; at 9 P.M., felt the same pain in the inner border of the right foot, then in the right arm, and then in the left side of the neck (first day), [2c] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Pains in the arms and hands (sixth day), [6] . ► Slight drawing in the right upper arm, after getting into bed, at 10 P.M., [2c] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Drawing pains, several times through the day, in the legs and back (first day), [4] . [250.] ► Aching in thighs and bend of knees (seventh day), [3] . ► Sharp lancinating pain in the hip-joint (third day), [5] . ► Dull aching pain in the hips and back (third day), [6] . ► Rheumatic pains in the shin bones, which causes the most excruciating pain in walking (eighth day), [6] . ► *Pain in the knees and ankles , aggravated by motion (after six hours), [6] . ► Feet and ankle very painful for the last forty-eight hours (seventh day), [6] . GENERALITIES. ► Great faintness accompanied the distress between the stomach and navel (fifth day), [1] . ► *Very weak and languid (third day), [1] . ► *Feeling greatly prostrated; can hardly stand up, my knees are so weak (fourth day), [1] . ► *So languid I can hardly walk (fifth day), [1] . [260.] ► *Very languid (sixth day), [1] . ► *Excessively weak (sixth day), [1] . ► Had to retire at dark, was so weak (sixth day), [1] . ► *Great lassitude with the severe aching in all the large joints (sixth day), [1] . ► *Feel weak and depressed, and indisposed to any exertion, physical or mental (sixth day), [2b] . ► General indefinable feeling of malaise, which lasted all day, [2c] . ► *Very languid and weak (second and ninth days), [5] . ► *Feel very languid and uneasy (fifth day), [6] . ► *Great debility at times (ninth day), [6] . ► *Feel weak and languid (eighth day), [3] . [270.] ► At times shuddering; rather nervous than from real chilliness (ninth day), [3] . ► *Aching in all the joints in the body and limbs (second day), [5] . ► Rheumatic and neuralgic pains, some of the time very severe (seventh day), [5] . ► 9 P.M., had the symptoms all day, but since 2 P.M., they have been getting easier (fourth day), [1] . SKIN. ► Itching over the whole body, most violent between the scapulæ and on the forearms (first day), [4a] . SLEEP. ► Disposition to yawn and stretch, with frequent chilliness along the spine, and hot flashes of fever (sixth day), [1] . ► *Disposition to yawn and stretch, with chilliness (second day), [3] . ► *Disposition to yawn and stretch (third day), [6] . ► Restless night (third and sixth nights), [1] . ► Restless and uneasy during the night (first night), [5] . [280.] ► *Sleep is disturbed by dreams, very restless and uneasy all night (second night), [6] . ► Frightful dreams of the water, [1a] . ► Dream much at night (fifth day), [4] . FEVER. ► Chilliness. ► *Unusual chilliness when the open air is encountered with icy coldness of the nose ; whether this last symptom was occasioned by the medicine or not, I cannot say, but I did not experience it before even during the extreme cold weather of this winter (seventh day), [3] . ► *Chilliness, with disposition to yawn and stretch (second day), [5] . ► *Chilliness and coldness over the whole body, lasting four hours (fourth day), [5] . ► *Chills creeping along the spine, intermingled with hot flashes (after four hours), [5] . ► Severe chills, lasted two hours, followed by fever and profuse perspiration (sixth day), [5] . ► Chills between the shoulders (ninth day), [5] . ► Frequent chilliness along the spine, and hot flashes of fever, with disposition to yawn and stretch (sixth day), [1] . [290.] ► While sitting in a warm room by a fire reading, I felt slight chilliness creep up the back to nape of the neck, most noticeable between the shoulder-blades; this was succeeded by a general feeling of chilliness, lasting for several minutes; for the moment it did not seem that this was the effect of the drug; I imagined the room was getting too cool, and rose to stir the fire, when the truth occurred to me: I mention this to show that this symptom was no imaginary one, caused by any expectation of such an effect (after one hour, sixth day); chilliness between the shoulder-blades (three hours later), [2b] . ► Coldness amounting almost to chills (third day), [6] . ► Coldness of nose (ninth day), [3] . ► Feet and hands cold (fourth day), [5] . ► Heat. ► High fever accompa

Great languor with severe aching pains in back, large joints and bones of legs ; yawns and stretches. Feels weak and depressed and indisposed to any exertion, physical or mental. Great debility at times ; can hardly stand up, knees are so weak. Periodical : headache, coming on at regular hour each day ; facial neuralgia. Alternate : fever and chill several times a day. Every half hour : passages of pure blood and a little mucus. Every day : at 12 M., burning pain in teeth ; lasts till midnight. Every other day : chills. Every month : sick-headache. Right : distress in lobe of liver. Left : pain in side of jaw ; pain in teeth and temples ; intermittent neuralgia side of head. Pain : in liver ; in abdomen, between stomach and navel ; in knees and ankles ; in lumbar vertebræ ; in back ; in bones ; in abdominal viscera. Severe pains : in arms and legs ; in back, large joints and bones of legs. Severe aching pains : in region of liver. Dragging pains : in liver. Colicky pains : in hypogastrium. Burning pain : in teeth ; in left temple. Burning distress : in stomach ; in epigastrium ; in umbilicus. Neuralgia : left side of head and temple. Dull aching pain : in head ; in back and hips. Dull pain : in frontal region. Distress : in region of umbilicus ; in hypogastric region ; in all large joints. Light, hollow feeling : in head. Coldness : of nose, hands and feet. Mr. R., æt. 35, bilious temperament ; facial neuralgia. Man, æt. 35, contracted disease two years ago while in the army ; ague. Lady, æt. 47, just passed change of life ; sick-headache.

mental_emotional.text: Low-spirited, gloomy, desponding, irritable. Absence of mind and loss of memory. Head feels light and hollow, with deep frontal headache and much faintness ; in malarial intermittents. Congestion of blood to head with vertigo.

head.text: Headache, dull, frontal, with fever. Dull aching pain in head. Headache with qualmishness. Sick-headache : from organic lesion of liver ; every month, with chilliness along spine. Headache in intermittent, remittent or bilious fevers. Periodical headache, coming on at regular hour each day.

face.text: Facial neuralgia, of a periodical character ; from torpid liver. Weak and languid ; poor appetite ; every day for the last week, at 12 M., burning pain in upper teeth, left side of jaw, also in left temple ; pain intense, lasts until midnight, when it gradually passes off ; feels it slightly through forenoon. θ Intermittent neuralgia. Intermittent neuralgia left side of head and temple.

mouth_throat.text: Taste : bitter, coppery, nauseous. Tongue : coated ; white ; yellow ; thickly yellow.

stomach_abdomen.text: APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Polyp.]. Perfect loss of appetite ; pale, anæmic ; chronic malaria. Sometimes has ravenous appetite ; but more often none. Desire for sour things, which relieve. Nausea and vomiting. Great faintness or all gone feeling in epigastric region. Burning distress in stomach, with dragging pains in liver. Severe aching pains in region of liver, extending over whole dorsal region ; portal congestion. Pain in liver, with jaundice of skin and great lassitude. Hepatic complaints, especially jaundice. Loud rumbling in bowels. Constant distress in region of umbilicus, with a feeling as if small intestines were being tied in knots ; summer diarrhœa. Pain in abdomen between stomach and navel.

stool_rectum.text: Loose stool. Sudden distress in hypogastric region, with great desire for stool ; light-colored, papescent stool, followed by dragging pains in liver and sinking in epigastrium. Stools of pure mucus, or mucus, blood and bile, with great faintness and distress in solar plexus after stool. Stools run from bowels with great force, composed of bile, mucus and black fecal matter, preceded by great burning pain and distress in epigastrium, right lobe of liver, and umbilicus and followed by symptoms of portal congestion. Lienteria ; stools undigested ; chronic anæmia. Dysentery, with intermittent fever, in a young lady ; passages of pure blood and a little mucus every half hour, with severe tenesmus and colicky pains in hypogastrium. Intermittent diarrhœa and dysentery. Chronic diarrhœa and dysentery. Constipation, dull headache and great languor.

urinary.text: Urine very high colored and scanty, bilious.

chest_heart.text: Phthisis, with copious night sweats and watery diarrhœa.

back.text: Severe headache. Dull aching pain in back and hips.

extremities.text: Pain in knees and ankles. Great aching distress in all the large joints. Severe pains in arms and legs.

modalities.text: REST. POSITION. MOTION. [35] [Polyp.]. Can hardly stand up, knees are so weak. Forenoon : neuralgia slight. Night : sweat ; restless ; uneasy ; fever and distress. After midnight : profuse sweat. Cannot bear open air : chilliness. Damp change : causes cold.

sleep.text: Disposition to yawn and stretch, with chilliness. Restless night ; sleep disturbed by dreams, very restless and uneasy all night.

fever_chill.text: Cannot bear the open air ; it makes him so chilly ; takes cold from least damp change. Chilliness, with disposition to yawn and stretch. Frequent creeping chills along spine, between shoulder-blades up back to nape of neck. Chills commencing in back, between shoulder blades. Coldness of nose, hands and feet. Chills creeping along spine, intermingled with hot flashes. Chill alternates with fever several times a day. Skin hot and dry ; especially palms of hands ; face hot and flushed. Very restless all night, with fever and aching distress in large joints. Sweat profuse after midnight. Extremities cold, bluish ; feeble pulse ; face bluish ; chills every other day, every succeeding attack being more severe ; gloomy and despondent, irritated at least trifle ; thick yellow coating on tongue ; no appetite ; constant nausea ; slight headache ; slight pain in lumbar vertebræ. θ Ague. Ague of two years duration, contracted while in army ; had been drugged with quinine, opium and mercurials ; chills, fever and sweat every other day ; great thirst between paroxysms ; very sick at stomach ; vomiting ; no appetite ; pain in back ; pain in bones ; pulse 115 ; restless and nervous. Chill light and short ; fever long and followed by perspiration ; in some cases perspiration is copious. Obstinate intermittent fevers < by exposure and neglect or abuse of quinine. Intermission very short ; almost continued fever. During apyrexia : headache ; bitter taste in mouth ; tongue coated white or yellow ; loss of appetite ; more or less pains in abdominal viscera, especially liver, with great lassitude. Intermittent, remittent and bilious fevers. Hectic chills and fever in phthisis, with copious night sweats.

_raw: Compare : Agaric., Bryon., Cinchon., Cornus, Gelsem., Ipec., Leptand., Nux vom., Podophyl
